name: TEXAS INSTRUMENTS INC
summary: Texas Instruments designs and manufactures semiconductors sold to electronics designers and manufacturers worldwide. Its two main segments are Analog (power management, signal chain products) and Embedded Processing (microcontrollers, processors, wireless connectivity, radar). With over 80,000 products, TI serves industrial, automotive, data center, personal electronics, and communications equipment markets. In 2025, TI generated $17.68 billion in revenue, with Analog accounting for 79% and Embedded Processing 15%.

products.name: Power Products
products.description: Battery-management solutions, DC/DC switching regulators, AC/DC regulators, power switches, linear regulators, voltage references for power management.

products.name: Signal Chain Products
products.description: Amplifiers, data converters, interface products, motor drives, clocks, logic and sensing products for real-world signal processing.

products.name: Microcontrollers
products.description: Self-contained systems with processor core, memory and peripherals designed to control specific tasks, often with integrated analog functionality.

products.name: Processors
products.description: Embedded processors designed for specific computing activity in embedded applications, including radar and vision processing.

products.name: Wireless Connectivity Products
products.description: Wireless connectivity semiconductors used primarily in industrial and automotive embedded processing applications.

products.name: DLP Products
products.description: Digital light processing products primarily used to project high-definition images, reported under the Other segment.

products.name: Calculators
products.description: Calculator products representing approximately 1% of TI revenue, reported under the Other segment.

products.name: ASICs
products.description: Application-specific integrated circuits, custom semiconductors reported under the Other segment.
